div.head: The Acting Secretary of State to the Minister in Czechoslovakia (Carr)
div.p: 82. Please explain to the Minister of Foreign Affairs either orally or in a personal note that inasmuch as this Government has taken the position that it could not pass on the merits of the European controversy, and inasmuch as it took no part in drawing up the Munich Agreement under which the Delimitation Commission is acting it could not appropriately intervene with the Commission as requested despite a desire on its part to give favorable consideration to any request received from the Czechoslovak Government.
